benchmark_scope: Eight-step pulsed scenario lifecycle campaign: idle -> ramp_up -> flat_top -> burn -> expansion -> dump -> recharge -> cool_down -> idle.
claim_boundary: Soft-isolated workstation timing evidence only. This report is not clean-room, target-hardware, HIL, PREEMPT_RT, or plant-deployment evidence.

environment.cpu_topology: 12 logical CPUs, 6 physical cores, one socket, SMT pairs 0/6 1/7 2/8 3/9 4/10 5/11
known_background_load: pytest on tests/test_server_contracts.py using about 141% CPU
rustc release build for SCPN-FUSION-CORE using about 99% CPU
mypy pre-commit worker using about 58% CPU
browser and development terminal processes active
environment.isolation_quality: soft-core-affinity only; contaminated by active workstation load

derived.interpretation: Rust is justified for hot-path/control-loop execution. Python remains suitable for control-plane orchestration and debugging. Tail outliers are dominated by workstation contamination, not by the scheduler contract alone.

commands.python: taskset -c 0 env PYTHONPATH=src python - <<'PY' ... focused in-process benchmark ... PY

follow_up: Repeat on a quiet workstation or isolated PREEMPT_RT host before release-grade claims.
Persist a first-class repository benchmark harness if the scheduler becomes a gating latency surface.
Expose or benchmark the PyO3 path separately if Python needs to call the Rust scheduler directly.
